utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: today i'm seeing christina cooper . her date of birth is 07/01/1954 . uh , ms. cooper is a new patient who was referred by diane nelson for a long-standing iron deficiency anemia .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm good , thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so tell me what brings you in today .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: recently i tried to donate blood , around december i think , and they told me i was anemic , which is something i've been dealing with for a while , so it's not the first time i've been told i'm anemic .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: or how have you been feeling in general with this ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not great . i have been feeling fatigued often during the day , and even feverish with chills at times . when i try to be active i like i ca n't catch my breath and i feel like i'm wheezing . i've had some headaches too , which is not like me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . are there any other symptoms ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been noting some chilling sensations . i also get cold so easily . it's annoying . i feel like i have to really bundle up . i do n't know if this is related but my anxiety and depression feel like it has been getting worse lately . i feel like a mess .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sounds like you're not feeling great , obviously . and i'm glad you came to see us . um , we're certainly going to try to figure this out and figure out what's going on , uh , but it sounds like you've been dealing with this anemia for a long time ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i've been anemic since i was 13 years old .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: right . so why do your doctors think you're anemic ? do you have a history of heavy periods ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well i did have heavy periods until i had a hysterectomy in 1996 . but no , they have not told me why they think i'm anemic , which is frustrating honestly .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . i can imagine that is . um , let's see if we can help though . since you had your hysterectomy your periods , of course , are no longer the issue . um , when was your last colonoscopy ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: about five to six years ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and was it relatively a normal exam ? did you have any polyps ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . they said they'd see me in 10 years .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well that's good news .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i agree .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , do you have a pacemaker or defibrillator , or have sleep apnea , or use oxygen at night ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . do you ever drink alcohol ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , but only once or twice a year .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . are you taking any supplements such as iron or vitamin b12 ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i already started taking my iron pills which i have not taken in about a year .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and what are you taking ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm taking 25 milligram tablets , twice daily .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and that's the , the ferrous sulfate ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that's it . i take one in the morning and one in the evening .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . anything else ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i take vitamin b12 , just the over the counter stuff .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , very good . all right , well let's go ahead and take a look and see what's going on .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: of course . you'll hear me , uh , talk through your exam so that i get all the information documented .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so use my general physical exam template . i will start by listening to your heart and lungs .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . next , i'd like you to lay back so i can examine your abdomen .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: is there any tenderness where i'm pressing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . you can sit up . so your physical exam is normal without any significant findings . all right ms. cooper , often when we initially see anemia and your host of symptoms , we suspect internal bleeding .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: is that why they want me to have another upper endoscopy ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: actually it would be an upper endoscopy and a colonoscopy , but yes , likely that's the reason why .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: lovely .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . unfortunately our cameras do not meet all the way in the middle , so if those tests back , come , if those tests come back fine , then we'll have you swallow a pill camera to take pictures as it moves through your , uh , system .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we may not need to , but it's just the first thing we can do to make sure that you're not losing blood . um , the second thing we can do is have you see a hematologist . they will tell us if you need to give any , to give you any intravenous iron , or maybe something to help your body store the iron better .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let's go ahead , get your upper endoscopy and colonoscopy scheduled .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , have you ever had any issue with sedation in the past ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i was just sleepy afterwards .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well we will give you a bowel prep to clean out your bowels ahead of time . um , if we do these tests and they are normal , like i said , then we will consider that capsule endoscopy .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . sounds like a plan .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so after that you'll be all done and we will send you to the hematologist . additionally , i'm going to need you to start taking your iron pills with orange juice . uh , the vitamin c will help you absorb the iron better . do this for about 8-12 weeks , uh , and then we can reassess your blood work .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that sounds great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . well i think we have our plan . on your way out , stop by and schedule your upper endoscopy and c- colonoscopy . uh , we will send a referral to dr. flores who is is the hematologist , so schedule that appointment . um , here are your instructions for the pre- uh , the bowel prep . uh , call us if you have any questions or worsening symptoms . we'll be happy to help you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome . have a great day , have a great day ms. cooper .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . this is christina cooper , pleasant 65 year old female who was diagnosed with iron deficiency anemia in 12-2019 , and w- and was unable to donate blood . um , her followup blood work on 01/20/20 was revealed a low hemoglobin , stable hematocrit and normal iron labs , although ferritin was low . um , she was taking ferrous sulfate , three hundred , twenty phil- 25 milligrams by mouth . i've asked her to continue each dose with vitamin c found in orange juice , for the next 12 weeks , then recheck to the cbc , iron , ferritin , b12 , and folate . um , a referral was sent to her hematologist . we will plan for an egd and a colonoscopy to assess for potential sources of anemia or gi bleed . if this is inconclusive , capsule endoscopy will be considered . thanks .

detail_list.summary: The doctor introduces the visit for a 65-year-old female patient with long-standing iron deficiency anemia.
The patient reports a history of anemia and an inability to donate blood in December 2019.
The patient reports fatigue, feverishness with chills, shortness of breath with activity, wheezing, and headaches.
The patient reports tingling sensations, cold sensitivity, and worsening anxiety and depression.
The patient has been anemic since age 13.
The doctor asks if the patient has a history of heavy menstrual periods.
The patient had heavy periods until a hysterectomy in 1996.
The doctor asks when the patient had her last colonoscopy.
The patient had a colonoscopy five to six years ago.
The patient reports the previous colonoscopy had nonsignificant findings.
The doctor asks if the patient has a pacemaker, defibrillator, sleep apnea, or uses oxygen.
The patient denies having a pacemaker, defibrillator, sleep apnea, or using oxygen.
The doctor asks about alcohol consumption.
The patient drinks alcohol one to two times per year.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking iron or vitamin B12 supplements.
The patient is taking ferrous sulfate 25 mg twice daily.
The patient takes over-the-counter vitamin B12.
The doctor declares an intent to perform a physical exam, starting with heart and lungs.
The doctor declares an intent to examine the abdomen.
The doctor asks if there is any abdominal tenderness.
The patient denies abdominal tenderness.
The doctor reports the physical exam is normal.
The doctor explains the need for an upper endoscopy and colonoscopy to investigate potential internal bleeding.
The doctor states that if endoscopy and colonoscopy are inconclusive, a capsule endoscopy will be considered.
The doctor states the patient will be referred to a hematologist for further evaluation.
The doctor asks if the patient has had issues with sedation in the past.
The patient denies past issues with sedation.
The doctor instructs the patient to take iron with orange juice for 12 weeks and then recheck blood work.
The doctor instructs the patient to schedule an upper endoscopy, colonoscopy, and hematology appointment.

case_description: iron deficiency anemia; chilling sensation; anxiety; depression
